summary:
A strain refers to a deformity caused by force application; it involves observable alterations like those seen in stressed mechanical components such as prosthetic heart valves undergoing cyclic loads. This definition encompasses both physical structures and biological entities responding to pressure-induced stresses. To summarize concisely for your query about defining 'strain' clinically within this narrow scope: 

1) It denotes structural change induced by loading,
2) Relevant across various contexts including biomechanics & physiology when assessing impacts of external influences upon integrity/stability. Agent2 concluded based on multiple sources indicating "deformation resulting from an applied load" accurately captures what constitutes strain medically. Key aspects include visible/quantifiable modifications post-force exposure applicable broadly—from engineered devices through cellular biology reactions triggered via environmental factors exerted therapeutically/internally/externally impacting health outcomes directly related mechanically/biologically thus fitting well into our understanding here regarding its core meaning per requirement
